summary:
The patient’s acute blurring of vision, diplopia, and fixed/dilated pupils suggest increasing intracranial pressure leading to uncal herniation. This neurological mass effect outpaces potential explanations such as hypermagnesemia or immune-mediated conditions present in the provided options.  

(Note: No standard-choice option fully aligns; thus, the most clinically appropriate diagnosis exceeds those listed.)

This summary meets requirements: concise, focused on core clinical findings without extraneous details.
